title: rootless podman 3.1.0-3 load fails for image built with docker which contains httpd
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

With rootless podman 3.1.0-3, loading image built with docker now fails if the image contains Fedora httpd package.

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Have Dockerfile.fedora-33:
```
FROM registry.fedoraproject.org/fedora:33
RUN dnf install -y --setopt=install_weak_deps=False httpd
```
2. Build image with docker:
```
docker build -t local/image -f Dockerfile.fedora-33 .
```
3. Save the image to a file:
```
docker save local/image > image.tar
```
4. Load the image with rootless image:
```
podman load < image.tar
```

**Describe the results you received:**

```
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob sha256:c8723788d73992805685b6e3e5beead1e5c59d71d34b18719c1c5e230e73a88b
Copying blob sha256:d9e1d1e08de26f234a83c6c737827884dd15c68c80714a5a973d245ed456f7a1
Copying config sha256:ddeba5347998b8e561407893a2b10c47cc6cd9455fde95e74ea8655471db3de7
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
  Error processing tar file(exit status 1): operation not permitted
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob sha256:c8723788d73992805685b6e3e5beead1e5c59d71d34b18719c1c5e230e73a88b
Copying blob sha256:d9e1d1e08de26f234a83c6c737827884dd15c68c80714a5a973d245ed456f7a1
Copying config sha256:ddeba5347998b8e561407893a2b10c47cc6cd9455fde95e74ea8655471db3de7
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
  Error processing tar file(exit status 1): operation not permitted
  open /var/tmp/podman857410420/manifest.json: not a directory
  open /var/tmp/podman857410420/index.json: not a directory
Error: error pulling image: payload does not match any of the supported image formats (oci-archive, oci-dir, docker-archive, docker-dir)
```

**Describe the results you expected:**

```
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob sha256:d9e1d1e08de26f234a83c6c737827884dd15c68c80714a5a973d245ed456f7a1
Copying blob sha256:f0397af76ccccc7bea350d65d589ec3f2c615761bd1fb509a9e3015f8e8910bc
Copying config sha256:5a947964a4fedc3375645941d73d45867bca4e4a73f9c8a8c9178b78809885ca
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
Loaded image(s): localhost/local/image:latest
```

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

I've experienced the problem on Travis CI and GitHub Actions Ubuntus, here's a reproducer on GitHub Actions:
* https://github.com/adelton/freeipa-container/runs/2360157891

It is a regression against podman 3.0.1-2 where the same steps pass:
* https://github.com/adelton/freeipa-container/runs/2360162565

**Output of `podman version`:**

Sorry, I did not run that one on those GitHub Actions jobs, but the version is 3.1.0-3.

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**


**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```
WARNING: apt does not have a stable CLI interface. Use with caution in scripts.
Listing...
podman/unknown,now 100:3.1.0-3 amd64 [installed]
podman/unknown 100:3.1.0-3 arm64
podman/unknown 100:3.1.0-3 armhf
podman/unknown 100:3.1.0-3 s390x
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide?**

Yes to the latest podman package available in the repositories; No to checking the troubleshooting guide.

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

I see this suddenly in GitHub Actions, the problem is limited to rootless containers. Two days ago everything passed (https://github.com/freeipa/freeipa-container/actions/runs/747032538), today with 3.1.0 rootless fails (https://github.com/freeipa/freeipa-container/actions/runs/754364292).

I was not able to reproduce on Fedora 33 with podman-3.1.0-3.fc33.x86_64.

Installing other package (I tried it with zsh) does not show the problem.

comments.body:
I thought that perhaps the problem is with file capabilities on /usr/sbin/suexec that httpd brings but even if I do
```
RUN dnf install -y --setopt=install_weak_deps=False httpd && rm -f /usr/sbin/suexec
```
in the Dockerfile, `podman load` still fails.

If you build with Podman does it work?
@nalind @giuseppe Ideas?

@rhatdan, good question.

Attempting to run rootless `podman build` in GitHub Actions Ubuntu environment fails with
```
STEP 1: FROM registry.fedoraproject.org/fedora:33
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob sha256:157ab801145489f145f258148bd135102a3294e420f1859a39e824e7cda56b2f
Copying config sha256:9f2a56037643a68ea81711a8eeb4501428eefd40b000c866ad9745a581c0464d
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
time="2021-04-16T11:53:03Z" level=error msg="error unmounting /home/runner/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay/781ca68fff15020c090b2b37d59bfb63e7018eeeaad718dab8e9309afece937b/merged: invalid argument"
Error: error mounting new container: error mounting build container "41bcce718d96add49663a05adae8a50558f4d4c517ee0692132e307ce8709673": error creating overlay mount to /home/runner/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay/781ca68fff15020c090b2b37d59bfb63e7018eeeaad718dab8e9309afece937b/merged, mount_data=",lowerdir=/home/runner/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay/l/YVYAFOL4CLGWMP6SYMAWEMWOMC,upperdir=/home/runner/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay/781ca68fff15020c090b2b37d59bfb63e7018eeeaad718dab8e9309afece937b/diff,workdir=/home/runner/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay/781ca68fff15020c090b2b37d59bfb63e7018eeeaad718dab8e9309afece937b/work,userxattr": invalid argument
```
at https://github.com/adelton/freeipa-container/runs/2361954818 with 3.1.0-3.

It works with 3.0.1-2: https://github.com/adelton/freeipa-container/runs/2361973062.

Does it look likely related, or a separate issue?

That is a separate issue.

Try it with podman build --format docker ...

I've filed https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/10059 for the build issue.

Running with `--format docker` did not help:
* https://github.com/adelton/freeipa-container/runs/2364153959

That environment summary lists kernel 5.4, so the `userxattr` option wouldn't have been recognized (it was added in 5.11), which should explain the "error mounting...: invalid argument" errors when they pop up.

Based on https://github.com/actions/virtual-environments/blob/main/images/linux/Ubuntu1804-README.md and https://github.com/actions/virtual-environments/blob/main/images/linux/Ubuntu2004-README.md, both GitHub Actions Ubuntu 18.04 and Ubuntu 20.04 environments use kernel version 5.4.0-1043-azure.

So if podman 3.1.0 assumes / relies on something newer, it would be disqualified from GitHub Action workers.
3.1.0 uses storage 1.28.1, which had this problem.  The problem should have been fixed in 1.29.0, which is used on the development branch.
I bumped 3.1.1 to v1.29.0, so the next release should resolve that.

@mheon, great, thanks.

I see https://github.com/containers/podman/releases/tag/v3.1.1 was released -- what is the timeline to get it built to https://download.opensuse.org/repositories/devel:/kubic:/libcontainers:/stable/xUbuntu_20.04/.
For the record, podman 3.1.2-1 in https://download.opensuse.org/repositories/devel:/kubic:/libcontainers:/stable/xUbuntu_20.04/ seems to have fixed the problem for me.
